relevant_files.role: Extension registry: EXTENSION_MAP and supported_languages() function
Language registration: get_language_info() and get_ts_language() match arms
Reference implementation: ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY, DEFUSE_QUERY patterns
Reference implementation: extract_inheritance handler and find_method_for_receiver
Feature flags and optional dependencies for language support
Workspace dependencies: tree-sitter version and language grammar versions
Integration test patterns for language support

conventions.commits: Conventional Commits with GPG signing and DCO sign-off (git commit -S --signoff); types: feat, fix, docs, refactor, test, chore; scope in parens; breaking changes marked with ! and BREAKING CHANGE footer
conventions.testing: AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert); one happy path and one edge case per behavior; unit tests in module under #[cfg(all(test, feature = "lang-xyz"))]; integration tests in crates/code-analyze-core/tests/; no redundant test variations
conventions.linting: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo deny check advisories licenses; cognitive complexity threshold 30; no .unwrap() in production code paths
conventions.error_handling: thiserror for libraries (ParserError enum); anyhow for applications; Result<T, E> return types; graceful degradation on unsupported languages

patterns: Language support via Cargo feature flags (lang-xyz) controlling conditional compilation of module, queries, and language info arms
Query constants (ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY, DEFUSE_QUERY) defined as &str at module level
Optional handler functions (extract_inheritance, find_method_for_receiver, extract_function_name, find_receiver_type) returning Option<T> or Vec<T>
Language registration in get_language_info() match arm returns LanguageInfo struct with all query and handler fields
Extension mapping in EXTENSION_MAP as slice of tuples (extension, language_name) with feature gate on each entry
Tree-sitter Language object obtained via tree_sitter_xyz::LANGUAGE.into() pattern
Query compilation cached in QUERY_CACHE to avoid per-file recompilation
Integration tests verify parse+extract, inheritance extraction, and analyze_symbol callees for each language

related_issues.title: feat(lang): add C# grammar support
related_issues.relevance: Closed language addition PR; demonstrates complete pattern for optional language support with feature flags, queries, handlers, and tests

related_issues.title: feat(lang): add Swift grammar support
related_issues.relevance: Open language addition issue; similar scope to Kotlin; may provide implementation insights or coordination opportunities

related_issues.title: feat: feature-flag tree-sitter language grammars to reduce binary size
related_issues.relevance: Closed issue establishing feature-flag architecture for optional language support; foundational pattern for Kotlin integration

related_issues.title: feat(languages): add Fortran language support
related_issues.relevance: Closed language addition; Fortran has no DEFUSE_QUERY (set to None), providing pattern for languages without def-use analysis

constraints: All languages must be feature-gated to allow compile-time reduction of binary size
Language info must be registered in both get_language_info() and get_ts_language() match arms, both feature-gated
Extension mapping must be feature-gated on each entry to prevent undefined language references
New language modules must include SPDX header (Apache-2.0) on line 1
All queries must compile and match valid tree-sitter syntax for the target grammar
Handler functions must gracefully handle edge cases (missing fields, empty nodes) without panicking
Tests must use #[cfg(all(test, feature = "lang-xyz"))] to avoid compilation failures when feature is disabled
test_coverage: Existing language tests follow AAA pattern with parse_xyz() helper function; each language has happy-path tests for ELEMENT_QUERY (class, function, method), CALL_QUERY, and extract_inheritance; integration tests in integration_tests.rs verify parse+extract and analyze_symbol callees; semantic_correctness.rs tests def-use analysis where DEFUSE_QUERY is present. Kotlin tests should follow same pattern: parse_kotlin() helper, test class/function/method/interface/enum/object extraction, test inheritance with delegation_specifiers, test imports, test calls.

library_findings.relevant_api: tree_sitter_kotlin::LANGUAGE constant; node kinds: class_declaration, function_declaration, interface_declaration, enum_class_declaration, object_declaration, call_expression, delegation_specifier, import_header, simple_identifier, type_identifier
library_findings.notes: fwcd/tree-sitter-kotlin is primary implementation (High reputation, 576 code snippets, 79.1 benchmark score). Kotlin AST structure mirrors Java with JVM-style class hierarchy. Key differences: delegation_specifiers for inheritance (no separate impl blocks), data/companion/object modifiers as part of declaration node, call_expression for method/function calls. No suspend modifier extraction needed for symbol analysis. Companion objects declared inline, no special extraction required.

library_findings.relevant_api: Parser, Language, Query, QueryCursor, Node; set_language(), parse(), matches(), captures for query execution
library_findings.notes: Core parsing library; version 0.26.6 pinned in workspace. Used by all language modules for AST traversal and query execution. Query API stable across versions.

approaches.name: Approach A: Full Kotlin support with DEFUSE_QUERY (recommended)
approaches.description: Add tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 to workspace dependencies. Create crates/code-analyze-core/src/languages/kotlin.rs with ELEMENT_QUERY (function_declaration, class_declaration, interface_declaration, enum_class_declaration, object_declaration with data/companion modifiers), CALL_QUERY (call_expression), REFERENCE_QUERY (type_identifier), IMPORT_QUERY (import_header), and DEFUSE_QUERY (identifier write/read sites). Implement extract_inheritance handler to walk delegation_specifiers and collect superclass (has constructor call with parens) and interface names (no parens). Add lang-kotlin feature flag to Cargo.toml, register .kt/.kts extensions in EXTENSION_MAP, add kotlin arm to get_language_info() and get_ts_language() in mod.rs. Write 8-10 unit tests covering class/function/interface/enum/object definitions, inheritance patterns, imports, and calls. Add integration tests for parse+extract and analyze_symbol. All tests pass with cargo test --features lang-kotlin.
pros: Provides complete symbol analysis including def-use tracking for Kotlin
Consistent with Java, C#, Python, Go, Rust, TypeScript, JavaScript, C++ implementations
Enables semantic correctness testing and def-use site analysis
Aligns with issue acceptance criteria which expects DEFUSE_QUERY (though not strictly required)
Maximizes value for users analyzing Kotlin codebases
cons: Requires careful query design to handle Kotlin-specific syntax (delegation_specifiers, data/companion modifiers)
DEFUSE_QUERY complexity may require iteration to capture all identifier write/read patterns
Slightly larger binary footprint when feature is enabled (minimal impact)

approaches.name: Approach B: Minimal Kotlin support without DEFUSE_QUERY
approaches.description: Add tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 to workspace dependencies. Create crates/code-analyze-core/src/languages/kotlin.rs with ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY only (DEFUSE_QUERY set to None in LanguageInfo). Implement extract_inheritance handler. Add lang-kotlin feature flag, register extensions, add kotlin arm to get_language_info() and get_ts_language(). Write 5-6 unit tests covering basic symbol extraction and inheritance. All tests pass with cargo test --features lang-kotlin.
pros: Simpler implementation: fewer queries to design and test
Follows Fortran pattern (no DEFUSE_QUERY) which is proven in production
Still provides symbol extraction, call analysis, and inheritance tracking
cons: No def-use site analysis for Kotlin (users cannot trace variable writes/reads)
Inconsistent with most other languages in the codebase (Java, C#, Python, Go, Rust, TypeScript, JavaScript, C++ all have DEFUSE_QUERY)
May require future enhancement if users request def-use analysis

approaches.name: Approach C: Kotlin support with deferred DEFUSE_QUERY (phased approach)
approaches.description: Implement Approach B (minimal support without DEFUSE_QUERY) in first PR. Add TODO comment in kotlin.rs noting DEFUSE_QUERY pattern to implement in follow-up PR. Create separate issue #650 (or similar) for DEFUSE_QUERY implementation. This allows Kotlin symbol analysis to ship immediately while deferring def-use analysis to future work.
pros: Unblocks Kotlin symbol analysis and call tracing in near term
Reduces scope and risk of initial PR
Allows community feedback on basic Kotlin support before investing in DEFUSE_QUERY
Clear path for future enhancement with tracked issue
cons: Requires two PRs instead of one
Users must wait for follow-up PR to get def-use analysis
May create perception of incomplete support

recommendation: Approach A (Full Kotlin support with DEFUSE_QUERY). Kotlin is a JVM-first language with extensive enterprise adoption (Android, microservices, Kotlin Multiplatform). The issue explicitly requests DEFUSE_QUERY in acceptance criteria. The codebase pattern is well-established (Java, C#, Python, Go, Rust, TypeScript, JavaScript, C++ all include DEFUSE_QUERY). tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 is mature (High reputation, 79.1 benchmark score) and provides clear node kinds for Kotlin syntax. The delegation_specifiers pattern for inheritance is straightforward to implement following the Java model. Medium complexity is justified by the value delivered: complete symbol analysis, def-use tracking, and consistency with the existing language portfolio. Estimated effort: 3-4 hours for implementation, testing, and review.
